Description: Checks whether X.509 certificate-based authentication (CBA) is enabled. CBA provides phishing-resistant authentication and is recommended for high-security environments alongside FIDO2.
Risk: Without certificate-based authentication, the tenant lacks an enterprise-grade phishing-resistant authentication method for scenarios where FIDO2 keys are impractical (e.g., smartcard-based authentication, VPN access, legacy systems). CBA complements FIDO2 for comprehensive phishing-resistant coverage.

Remediation.Recommendation: Enable X.509 certificate-based authentication in the Entra ID authentication methods policy. Configure trusted certificate authorities and certificate-to-user mapping rules.

SourceNotes: Certificate-based auth is a phishing-resistant method alongside FIDO2.
